text:
At least 16 oil tankers, cargo and other commercial ships have been attacked in the Persian Gulf since the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran began nearly two weeks ago, a New York Times analysis shows. The International Energy Agency warned that the war had caused “the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.”
Where ships have been attacked
Advertisement
SKIP ADVERTISEMENTThe Israeli military heavily bombarded central Beirut on Thursday evening, saying it was targeting infrastructure belonging to the militant group Hezbollah. Iraq and Oman closed oil terminals after two tankers were attacked and left burning off Iraq’s coast; they are among at least 16 oil tankers and cargo ships that have been attacked in the Persian Gulf since the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran began nearly two weeks ago, a New York Times analysis shows.
title: The Strait of Hormuz is narrow — and shallow enough to lay minefields.
